REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO, AND MOTION TO STRIKE CERTAIN PORTIONS OF , PLTF'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE NUMBER OF DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, WHICH IS 20 DAYS AS STIPULATED BY THE PARTIES: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS PLEADED AN EXPRESS WARRANTY BUT SEEKS TO DEFEND ITS PLEADING BY JUDICIARY NOTICE OF A PRIVACY POLICY. THE COURT CANNOT TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THAT POLICY BUT IF IT DID, THE TERMS WOULD CONTRADICT PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST EITHER PLEAD THE LANGUAGE OF THE ALLEGED WARRANTY OR ATTACH IT TO AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MR. LILIENSTEIN WAIVED THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
JIMMY SANH L. LY, (LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP), (415)362-2580, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.
DAVID M. LILIENSTEIN, ESQ., (415)271-7169, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, SYLVESTER HARRIS

THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE NUMBER OF DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, WHICH IS 20 DAYS AS STIPULATED BY THE PARTIES: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS PLEADED AN EXPRESS WARRANTY BUT SEEKS TO DEFEND ITS PLEADING BY JUDICIARY NOTICE OF A PRIVACY POLICY. THE COURT CANNOT TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THAT POLICY BUT IF IT DID, THE TERMS WOULD CONTRADICT PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST EITHER PLEAD THE LANGUAGE OF THE ALLEGED WARRANTY OR ATTACH IT TO AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MR. LILIENSTEIN WAIVED THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB/BD)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. DAVID M. LILIENSTEIN OF THE DL LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 271-7169 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ANNE MICHELLE BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-1403 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY THE COMMISIONER OF THE CA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT ALREADY GAVE THE PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND HIS COMPLAINT AND THERE IS NOTHING PRESENTED TODAY TO SUGGEST ANY WAY AROUND IT AND AROUND THE FAILINGS OF THIS COMPLAINT . WHILE THE COURT UNDERSTANDS THAT LIBERAL LEAVE TO AMEND IS GIVEN UNDER THE LAW AND THAT IT HAD, THE CIRCUMSTANCE JUST DON'T EXIST TO JUSTIFY IT HERE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR# 7489 =301/PJB/AL
